steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 1 says Tang's ambitions 'weren't very lofty' — he built the site just because everyone had one. Sentence 2 says the site 'has become an essential resource' for professionals. The second sentence contradicts the first: a low-ambition origin led to a high-impact outcome. The transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Nevertheless,": signals contrast despite what came before. Low ambition; nevertheless, the site became essential. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • "Therefore,": signals a logical consequence. Low ambition does not logically cause a site to become essential — the outcome is surprising, not predictable. ✗
  • "Furthermore,": signals addition of a similar point. The second sentence contradicts, not adds to, the first. ✗
  • "Next,": signals a time sequence or list of steps. The passage is making a contrasting observation, not narrating a series of events. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
